name: fabric-user-data-functions

metadata.name: fabric-user-data-functions
metadata.summary: This package contains bindings and middle ware required for fabric functions built on python.

documentation:
# Fabric Functions Python Worker

## Introduction

This project contains the necessary bindings and middleware we can register on a python function in order to receive fabric data from our worker extension. 

## Fabric Item Binding

By importing the `fabric_item_input` binding, the user can add a new attribute to their fabric function. This input has the same properties as the FabricItem attribute and binding from the host extension and will pass down to the worker the information to create a FabricItem:

classes.name: fabric.functions.middleware.invocationIdMiddleware.InvocationIdMiddleware
documentation: A Python worker extension to add the invocation id to the response headers.

modules.name: fabric.functions.internal.udf_string
documentation: Since str can't have a binding different than the Generic binding, we change its annotation to this class so it can be bound differently

classes.name: fabric.functions.internal.udf_binding.UdfPropertyConverter
documentation: The input converter that automatically gets registered in the function app.

classes.name: fabric.functions.internal.user_data_function_context_binding.UserDataFunctionContextConverter
documentation: The input converter that automatically gets registered in the function app.

methods.name: fabric.functions.user_data_functions.UserDataFunctions.connection
documentation: The decorator that will be used to tell the function we want a fabric item

classes.name: fabric.functions.internal.item_binding.FabricItemInput
documentation: The binding object that will be used by our input decorator below

classes.name: fabric.functions.internal.item_binding.FabricItemConverter
documentation: The input converter that automatically gets registered in the function app.
